appraisal_events.evidence: « grâce à sa sensibilité frémissante, à sa timidité craintive et vite affolée, Saniette leur offrait un souffre-douleur quotidien... on avait soin de l'inviter avec des paroles aimables... à seules fins alors de le chatouiller et de lui faire des brimades »
appraisal_events.explanation: The narrator exposes a deliberate pattern where the Verdurins keep Saniette close only to bully him, marking him as a standing victim within the group.

appraisal_events.evidence: « dans ces milieux bourgeois et artistes... [il] passait pour l'incarnation même de l'inversion… sa grande situation mondaine, sa haute origine, étaient entièrement ignorées… on le confondait avec un comte Leblois… »
appraisal_events.explanation: Outside his own milieu, gossip and mistaken identity attach scandal to Charlus and erase his pedigree; the narrator reports this while showing it to be largely erroneous.

status_effects.explanation: He is positioned as the group's daily scapegoat, included only to be humiliated, which sharply lowers his local standing.
In the bourgeois/artistic milieu around the Verdurins, rumor-driven confusion tarnishes his reputation and obscures his rank.

ambiguities: The social discredit aimed at baron de Charlus is reported as widespread but is explicitly traced to confusion and rumor, so the narrator distances himself from its validity.
